name: Baguette Omelet Sandwich
description: It’s a baguette sandwich made even better with an omelet inside.

steps: Today’s dish is an omelet sandwich made with a baguette. Slice the tomato.
Spinach.
Parsley, my tiny green broom.
Cut it roughly.
I need garlic...one??. I need more!!.
Chop. chop.
I will make the garlic butter spread. 30g of Butter.
1TBSP minced garlic, 1TBSP of honey.
1/2TBSP minced parsley.
Cut off the top of the baguette like a lid.
Scoop out the inside of the bread to make space.
Apply the garlic butter spread inside the bread.
4-5 Cute eggs (depending on the size of the baguette).
You can put eggs straight into baguette (I used a bowl for thumbnail~).
Put eggs in the baguette (Move it onto the baking tray before adding eggs).
A little Salt.
A little Pepper. You can beat the eggs roughly or cook them as they are (up to your taste).
Parsley. To keep it from burning, I covered the baguette with aluminum foil.
Cook it in the oven pre-heated to 190°C for 5-7 min. My oven isn’t very strong, so I made a hole to cook it faster (Should’ve done it from the start).
I baked it more at 200°C for 5-7min (Adjust the time & temperature).
Your favorite cheese Tomato and cheese.
Ham. Mozzarella.
A little~. Spinach.
And this time, a lot of Mozzarella~. Trim the thick part of the bread lid to make it flat.
Spread the garlic butter on top. Cover it with the bread lid and put it in the oven.
Just melt the mozzarella for 5-8 min in a 190-200°C preheated oven (Adjust). Delicious garlic-butter scent and the super crispy baguette.
